message:
fix(pager): suppress stale commit publishes that would clobber a newer visible snapshot

Add `should_skip_stale_publish` and route every commit-publish path
through it so a late-arriving smaller / older commit cannot overwrite
the page plane or db-size for a snapshot that the publisher has
already advanced past. The new helper compares the incoming commit's
sequence and snapshot generation against the currently published
state and returns early when:

- the incoming commit_seq is strictly less than the published one
  (a slow writer racing a fast follow-up commit on the same connection),
- the incoming snapshot generation is older than the visible one
  (an out-of-order publish from a background task), or
- the incoming page set + db_size combination would shrink a snapshot
  that downstream readers are already observing as larger.

Seven new tests cover the regressions this prevents:

  - `test_single_connection_metadata_only_commit_clears_stale_page_plane_before_later_publish`
    A metadata-only commit must wipe stale page-plane entries so the
    next page publish on the same commit_seq cannot resurrect them.
  - `test_stale_smaller_commit_publish_does_not_mutate_newer_snapshot`
  - `test_read_page_copy_zero_fills_pages_beyond_db_size_even_if_cache_is_stale`
    Reads past the published db_size must zero-fill rather than serve
    a stale cached page from a previous-larger snapshot.
  - `test_publish_truncate_checkpoint_updates_published_db_size_on_same_commit_seq`
    A truncate publish on the same commit_seq is special-cased so the
    db_size shrink takes effect even though the seq did not advance.
  - `test_stale_larger_commit_publish_does_not_reopen_newer_shrunken_snapshot`
  - `test_metadata_only_publish_without_page_clear_advances_snapshot_generation`
    Metadata-only publishes still bump the generation so subsequent
    readers correctly invalidate any cached references to the old gen.
  - `test_metadata_only_publish_without_page_clear_preserves_newer_visible_snapshot`

These collectively pin the contract: snapshot visibility advances
monotonically per (commit_seq, generation), and stale publishes are
silently dropped instead of corrupting the view.
